adapted_from.boundary: Transfer the diagnostic method only. Do not import OR research-domain objects, paper-specific stages or project assumptions.

purpose: Give diagnostics a machine-readable layer matrix so root cause is assigned by bottom-up evidence, not by the location where the symptom appeared.

trace_rule.rule: Start at the observed failure surface. Build a lower evidence track and an upper principle track side by side. Promote a meta or constitutional cause only when lower evidence justifies it.
trace_rule.stop_condition: Stop climbing at the earliest layer that can prevent recurrence without relying on downstream cleanup.
trace_rule.anti_pattern: Do not assign the owning repair layer merely because a symptom appeared in a file, Canvas view, script output or user screenshot.

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: What command, report, artifact, Canvas view, user correction or validation result proves the failure?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: What kind of reliability, truth, boundary or closure risk does this expose?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Is the visible artifact itself wrong, stale, malformed, incomplete or misleading?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Did the system treat prose, layout or local files as truth without a governed validity state?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Which skill, script, generator, route, workflow or manual step produced the surface?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Did role authority, owner boundary or operating mode invite the wrong action?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Did a validator, review gate, visual audit, screenshot audit or closure check miss or falsely pass the defect?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Is the evaluation observing the wrong signal or accepting incomplete evidence?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Did the producer consume stale, incomplete, ambiguous or boundary-violating registry, schema, source record, catalog, route, context pack or Canvas graph data?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Is the data/control-plane split weak or is the evidence ontology missing a needed type?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Is the source, registry, route, information map, module map, taxonomy, loop state or project ontology wrong or missing?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Is the collaboration contract, project identity, authority model or lifecycle model underspecified?

bottom_up_steps.lower_evidence_question: Do several lower surfaces point to the same organizing mistake?
bottom_up_steps.upper_principle_question: Should the system objective, evidence ontology, authority model, state model, failure philosophy or learning constitution be changed?

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: The system optimizes for producing artifacts, passing gates or moving the loop instead of preserving knowledge truth and boundary integrity.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: State that evidence sufficiency, source fidelity, machine truth and boundary integrity outrank throughput.

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: The system lacks first-class types for source fact, extraction, transformation, inference, visual projection, review finding, diagnosis and repair proposal.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: Define the missing object type and forbid silent promotion between evidence classes.

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: The system lacks a hierarchy for who may define truth, interpret truth, write prose, accept outputs, change gates or promote rules.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: Declare owner layers and permission boundaries for the affected module or workflow.

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: Artifacts are treated as loose files instead of governed state with provenance, status, dependencies and invalidation.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: Add manifests, statuses, dependency edges, lifecycle values or invalidation rules.

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: The system fails open when evidence is missing, stale, ambiguous, out of boundary or visually unverified.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: Make unsafe states blocked, pending, review_required or diagnosis_required until the owner layer repairs them.

constitutional_root_layers.failure_pattern: Corrections remain as chat memory or local patches instead of becoming method, gate, regression sample, queue state or closure evidence.
constitutional_root_layers.durable_response: Route reusable lessons through diagnosis, review, loop, registry, validator and closure surfaces.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: No clear standard for evidence, source fidelity, visual acceptance or accepted quality.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Add a quality contract, review criterion or acceptance gate before artifact production.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: Skills or modules overlap, or data/control planes are blurred.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Split ownership and route fields, files, views or workflows to the correct layer.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: The agent optimizes for immediate progress, patches locally or acts before diagnosis.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Add a diagnosis-first gate, handoff boundary or self-audit trigger.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: Machine gates check the wrong signal or miss the user-visible failure mode.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Add or recalibrate validators, review gates, screenshot evidence, regression checks or closure reports.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: Reports do not show the state needed to diagnose the failure.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Add state snapshots, provenance, machine-readable reports, route surfaces or visual projections.

meta_root_layers.failure_pattern: Encoding, paths, shell policy, tool limits or local environment pressure distort behavior.
meta_root_layers.durable_response: Fix shared runtime routes or make the limitation explicit in the validator, route or tool map.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: User says the diagnosis was shallow or missed the higher-layer problem.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Open diagnostic_self_audit, update the layer matrix or self-audit policy, then re-run affected validation.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: A Canvas graph looks wrong after registry or policy changed.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Update the visual projection and support bindings, then run visual and screenshot audits.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: A route, information-map entry or module closure is stale.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Repair the owning machine truth first, then sync docs, Canvas and validators.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: A new module or subsystem ignores old content.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Run legacy asset audit and record adopt, migrate, deprecate, archive, exempt or queue actions.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: Validation passes but the user-visible experience is still wrong.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Treat the validation as incomplete evidence, add rendered or user-visible acceptance evidence, and diagnose the gate.

symptom_to_owner.symptom: A repeated issue produces local fixes without a durable improvement path.
symptom_to_owner.repair_direction: Open the failure-learning loop and create postmortem, proposal, regression guard and promotion decision objects when the risk warrants it.

local_repair_allowed_when: The defect is confined to one artifact.
No reusable rule, route, schema, registry, validator, review gate or visual projection is missing.
Downstream consumers cannot silently reuse the faulty pattern.
The final response names the repair as local.
system_upgrade_required_when: The defect can recur across views, modules, routes, schemas, workflows, sources, indexes, agents or future projects.
A validator, review gate, route, schema, registry, Canvas policy or closure map allowed the defect.
User correction concerns boundary, ownership, method, trust or reliability.
A local patch would be invisible to future agents.
blocked_investigation_required_when: The owner layer cannot be identified from available evidence.
Fixing now would require changing a sibling module without reading it.
The proposed repair would mix human explanation with machine truth.
Required source evidence or local context is missing.

closure_questions: What changed so the next same-class task follows the new method?
Which owner layer now contains the rule or positive model?
Which report, validator, screenshot, review record or closure proof shows this is not hand-patched?
Which same-class assets and sibling surfaces were checked?
Which downstream consumers were repaired or explicitly unaffected?
Does the failure need a learning-loop postmortem or improvement proposal beyond the issue queue?
